decisions.title: Block 4 targets dataplane multiscope parity, not generic smoke parallelism
decisions.description: The remaining gap is in consumer and emulator bootstrap/runtime behavior. Configctl and validator already support multiscope runtime state; Block 4 will harden the dataplane to match that reality while keeping raccoon-cli as the proof engine.
alternatives: Extend smoke identity again without changing runtime behavior
Introduce new ad hoc runtime APIs just for smoke

decisions.title: Preserve global/default as operator baseline while removing it as the only supported bootstrap path
decisions.description: Consumer and emulator configs can keep global/default as a simple local default, but the dataplane must no longer require a single fixed scope to function correctly. The plan should evolve bootstrap/runtime behavior without breaking baseline local workflows.
alternatives: Keep global/default as a hard runtime requirement

decisions.title: Use the existing runtime ingestion bindings endpoint as the aggregate bootstrap seam
decisions.description: The bootstrap seam for Block 4 does not require a new HTTP API. ListActiveIngestionBindings already supports empty scope queries, so runtimebootstrap can expose an explicit aggregate bootstrap path while preserving the current scope-specific path for troubleshooting and staged rollout.
alternatives: Create a new aggregate-only endpoint on server/configctl
Immediately replace all scope-specific bootstrap flows

decisions.title: Consumer and emulator now default to aggregate bootstrap at startup
decisions.description: The dataplane now uses the aggregate runtime bootstrap seam by default at startup. Consumer and emulator no longer depend on a single configured scope for initial binding discovery, while the scope-specific bootstrap path remains available inside runtimebootstrap for troubleshooting and staged rollout.
alternatives: Keep startup on scope-specific bootstrap and postpone the wiring change
Replace the scope-specific bootstrap path entirely

decisions.title: Use aggregate bootstrap polling for continuous dataplane refresh in Block 4
decisions.description: Consumer and emulator now refresh the aggregate bootstrap continuously and only react when the binding-set signature changes. This keeps the refresh path aligned with the existing runtimebootstrap seam and avoids introducing new config event consumers before the dataplane runtime model is fully hardened.
alternatives: Add dedicated NATS config event consumers for consumer/emulator in this cut
Keep dataplane on startup-only aggregate bootstrap and postpone refresh

decisions.title: Block 4 closes with aggregate bootstrap plus polling refresh as the canonical dataplane model
decisions.description: This iteration closes the main multiscope parity gap by moving consumer and emulator to aggregate bootstrap and signature-driven refresh. Event-driven reconciliation remains a possible future refinement, but it is no longer required to remove the single-scope dataplane dependency.
alternatives: Delay closure until NATS event-driven reconciliation exists
Keep startup-only aggregate bootstrap without continuous refresh
